title: Priming System Fixes
description: Fix priming chat method filtering, state persistence, fit button, method output, and graph tool integration. Each story runs independently with dev-browser verification.

stories.title: Filter priming methods to pure-priming only
stories.description: Remove methods that require learner input (answering questions or hand-drawing) from the priming method picker. Update PRIME_METHOD_DISPLAY_ORDER in TutorWorkflowPrimingPanel.tsx to contain exactly these 8 methods: M-PRE-004, M-PRE-006, M-PRE-008, M-PRE-009, M-PRE-010, M-PRE-012, M-PRE-013, M-PRE-014. Remove M-PRE-002 (Pre-Question Set, requires answering questions) and M-PRE-005 (Skeleton Concept Hierarchy, requires building concept map). The constant is at approximately line 39-50 of TutorWorkflowPrimingPanel.tsx.
acceptanceCriteria: M-PRE-002 does not appear in the priming method picker
M-PRE-005 does not appear in the priming method picker
Exactly 8 methods appear: M-PRE-004, M-PRE-006, M-PRE-008, M-PRE-009, M-PRE-010, M-PRE-012, M-PRE-013, M-PRE-014
Method cards still show energy cost, duration, and description
dev-browser: open priming panel, verify only 8 methods listed, confirm M-PRE-002 and M-PRE-005 are absent

stories.title: Remove Hand-Drawn Map from priming
stories.description: M-ENC-015 (Hand-Drawn Map) is an ENCODE-phase method but appears in priming UI via PRIME_HAND_DRAW_METHOD_IDS in brain/dashboard/api_tutor_workflows.py (approximately line 54). Remove M-ENC-015 from the priming panel entirely. Check TutorWorkflowPrimingPanel.tsx for any frontend references to M-ENC-015 or hand_draw_map and remove those too. Ensure no console errors after removal.
acceptanceCriteria: M-ENC-015 does not appear in the priming method picker
M-ENC-015 does not appear in any priming chat output
PRIME_HAND_DRAW_METHOD_IDS no longer contains M-ENC-015
No console errors related to missing hand-draw references
dev-browser: open priming panel, search page text for 'Hand-Draw' or 'Hand Draw', confirm absent

stories.title: Persist chat state through panel drag and resize
stories.description: Chat messages, input text, and run results disappear when the user drags or resizes the priming panel. Root cause: WorkspacePanel.tsx uses positionResetToken in the Rnd component key (line ~327): key={`expanded:${String(positionResetToken ?? 'stable')}`}. When panelTransformResetToken changes (triggered by Maximize, Center, viewport focus in StudioShell.tsx line ~1058), the Rnd key changes, React unmounts/remounts everything inside, destroying useState. But the user reports it happens on ANY drag or resize, not just Maximize/Center. Investigate whether onDragStop or onResizeStop also triggers a token change or state reset. Fix: Lift priming chat state (chatTurns, chatInput, sendingChat, and current run result) out of local useState into a persistent store (zustand recommended, keyed by workflow/session ID). The store must survive Rnd remounts. Chat state in TutorWorkflowPrimingPanel.tsx is at approximately lines 1073-1076: chatTurns, sendingChat, chatInput as useState hooks.
acceptanceCriteria: Run a priming method, get chat results with multiple messages
Drag the priming panel to a new position -- all chat messages remain visible
Resize the priming panel -- all chat messages remain visible
Click Maximize -- all chat messages remain visible
Click Center -- all chat messages remain visible
Type partial text in chat input, drag panel, text remains in input
dev-browser: run a method, count messages, drag panel title bar 200px right, count messages again, assert same count; resize panel, count messages again, assert same count

stories.title: Fix Fit button for all panels
stories.description: The Fit button on panel title bars does not resize panels to fit their content. measureFitContentSize() in WorkspacePanel.tsx (lines ~205-282) temporarily removes overflow constraints and measures scrollWidth/scrollHeight, but returns incorrect dimensions for panels with flex or absolute-positioned content. The function relies on contentScrollRef and contentMeasureRef. Max constraints are 1400x1000 with 24px horizontal and 64px vertical chrome padding (lines ~80-83). Fix the measurement to produce correct content dimensions for all layout types. Consider measuring actual rendered child element bounding rects as a fallback when scroll dimensions match the container size (indicating the content is not naturally flowing). The Fit button shows because canFitContent (line ~152) is true when onSizeChange is passed, which it is from StudioShell (line ~1636).
acceptanceCriteria: Click Fit on priming panel with chat content -- panel resizes to show all content without scrolling
Click Fit on document dock with a document loaded -- panel resizes appropriately
Click Fit on notes panel -- panel resizes to fit note content
Panel does not exceed 1400px wide or 1000px tall
Panel does not shrink below its minWidth/minHeight
dev-browser: open priming panel, get its size, click Fit button, get new size, assert size changed; repeat for at least one other panel type

stories.title: Fix empty output for Big Picture and Terminology methods
stories.description: M-PRE-013 (Big-Picture Orientation Summary) and M-PRE-012 (Terminology Pretraining) produce empty or blank output when run in the priming chat. Investigate root cause by: (1) Reading the facilitation_prompt in sop/library/methods/M-PRE-012.yaml and M-PRE-013.yaml -- check if prompts are well-formed with clear instructions. (2) Checking brain/chain_runner.py to see how the facilitation_prompt is sent to the LLM. (3) Checking brain/dashboard/api_tutor_workflows.py for the method run endpoint -- verify source material context is being passed. (4) Check if output parsing in the frontend (TutorWorkflowPrimingPanel.tsx) drops or ignores these methods' output blocks. Fix at the source -- whether that is the YAML prompt, backend context passing, or frontend parsing.
acceptanceCriteria: Run M-PRE-012 Terminology Pretraining -- produces a list of key terms with definitions extracted from source material
Run M-PRE-013 Big-Picture Orientation Summary -- produces a meaningful summary orienting the learner to the big picture of the material
Output appears in priming chat as formatted content blocks, not empty or generic filler text

dev-browser: select M-PRE-012, click run, wait for response, verify chat contains non-empty text with at least 3 terms; select M-PRE-013, click run, wait for response, verify chat contains non-empty summary text

stories.title: Add graph tool choice for map-producing priming methods
stories.description: When a priming method produces a concept map or graph (detected as a mermaid code block), show a choice instead of only rendering inline. Add two buttons: 'View as Text' (shows ASCII/text version inline in chat) and 'Open in Graph Tool' (opens the mermaid content in the ConceptMapStructured panel). Currently, mermaid detection happens in TutorWorkflowPrimingPanel.tsx around line ~560-568 where block.kind === 'concept_map' triggers an inline ConceptMapStructured embed. Replace this with a choice UI. The ConceptMapStructured panel can be opened via the existing studio panel system. If the panel is not already open, auto-open it. Use the existing extractMermaidBlock utility and the ConceptMapStructured component's initialMermaid prop. Style buttons consistently with the priming panel design (rounded-full, font-mono, uppercase tracking).
acceptanceCriteria: When a priming result contains a mermaid block, two buttons appear: 'View as Text' and 'Open in Graph Tool'
Clicking 'View as Text' shows the text representation inline in chat
Clicking 'Open in Graph Tool' opens the ConceptMapStructured panel with the mermaid content loaded
If ConceptMapStructured panel is closed, it auto-opens when 'Open in Graph Tool' is clicked
Buttons are styled consistently with priming panel design
dev-browser: run a method that produces a concept map (like M-PRE-008), verify two buttons appear with correct labels, click 'Open in Graph Tool', verify a graph panel opens with rendered nodes
